title: odontites viscosus subsp. hispanicus
intro: Odontites viscosus subsp. hispanicus, also known as sticky toothwort, is a rare plant species belonging to the family Orobanchaceae, which is a type of parasitic plant that obtains its nutrients by drawing them from the surrounding environment. The sticky toothwort has been found to possess medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which make it useful in treating various health issues. Its health benefits include the potential treatment of wounds, skin irritations, and respiratory problems, as well as its ability to reduce fever and alleviate pain. In herbal preparations, the sticky toothwort is typically used in the form of infusions, decoctions, or salves, and can also be dried and used as a powder or added to ointments and creams.
